userStories.title: Rewrite planCommand to output prd.json instead of spec.md
userStories.description:
Rewrite `src/cli/plan.ts` `planCommand()` to output a valid `prd.json` instead of a spec markdown file.

Changes:

1. Update `planCommand()` signature: accept `--from <spec-path>` (required), `--auto` flag, `-f <feature>` (required), `-b <branch>` (optional, default `feat/<feature>`).

2. Remove the old `SPEC_TEMPLATE` and `buildPlanPrompt()`. Replace with `buildPlanningPrompt()` that includes:
   - Spec content (read from `--from` path)
   - Codebase context (from `scanCodebase()`)
   - Output schema (exact `prd.json` JSON structure with field descriptions)
   - Complexity classification guide (simple/medium/complex/expert definitions)
   - Test strategy guide (test-after/tdd-lite/three-session-tdd recommendations)

3. Output path changes from `config.plan.outputPath` (spec.md) to `nax/features/<feature>/prd.json`.

4. In `--auto` mode: send one-shot prompt via `adapter.complete()`, parse JSON response, validate against PRD schema, write file.

5. JSON validation: use the existing `UserStory` type shape — ensure all required fields present, complexity is valid enum, dependencies reference valid story IDs, status is always `"pending"`.

6. Auto-detect `project` from `package.json` name or git remote. Auto-generate `branchName` as `feat/<feature>` unless `-b` overridden.

7. Add `createdAt` and `updatedAt` ISO timestamps to output.

Do NOT implement interactive mode yet (PLN-002 handles that). Throw clear error if `--auto` is not specified ("Interactive mode not yet implemented, use --auto").
acceptanceCriteria: AC-1: planCommand reads spec from --from path and includes content in prompt
AC-2: Planning prompt includes codebase context, output schema, complexity guide, and test strategy guide
AC-3: In --auto mode, adapter.complete() is called with the full planning prompt
AC-4: JSON response is parsed and validated — invalid JSON or missing required fields throws clear error
AC-5: Output written to nax/features/<feature>/prd.json with correct structure
AC-6: All story statuses are forced to 'pending' regardless of LLM output
AC-7: project field auto-detected from package.json or git remote
AC-8: branchName defaults to feat/<feature>, overridable via -b flag

routing.reasoning: Major rewrite of planCommand with new prompt construction, JSON parsing/validation, and schema enforcement. Multiple integration points (scanner, adapter, PRD schema). Needs careful prompt engineering for reliable JSON output.

userStories.title: Interactive planning mode via ACP session
userStories.description:
Implement the default interactive planning mode for `nax plan` using ACP persistent sessions.

Changes:

1. In `planCommand()`, when `--auto` is NOT specified (default), use ACP interaction bridge for a conversational planning session.

2. Flow:
   a. Read spec from `--from`
   b. Scan codebase
   c. Start ACP session via adapter (use `adapter.run()` with planning prompt)
   d. Agent reads spec + codebase context, asks clarifying questions
   e. Questions detected by interaction bridge → forwarded to human (CLI stdin or interaction plugin)
   f. Human answers → sent as follow-up prompts to same session
   g. Agent produces final `prd.json` JSON output
   h. Parse, validate, write to `nax/features/<feature>/prd.json`

3. Wire the existing `interactionBridge` in `PlanOptions` — it already has `detectQuestion` and `onQuestionDetected` but is only used for CLI stdin. Extend to support the ACP interaction bridge from the adapter.

4. Final output extraction: agent's last message should contain a JSON code block with the PRD. Parse it using the same validation as `--auto` mode.

5. Add timeout: planning session has a configurable timeout (default 10 min). If exceeded, extract whatever partial output exists and warn.

Depends on PLN-001 for the prompt construction and JSON validation logic.
acceptanceCriteria: AC-1: Default nax plan (no --auto) starts an interactive ACP session
AC-2: Agent asks clarifying questions that are forwarded to human via interaction bridge
AC-3: Human responses are sent as follow-up prompts to the same ACP session
AC-4: Final output is extracted from agent's last message as JSON
AC-5: Output validated and written to nax/features/<feature>/prd.json
AC-6: Planning session respects timeout (default 10 min)
AC-7: CLI stdin interaction works for local terminal usage

routing.reasoning: Async interaction bridge wiring with ACP sessions, question detection, timeout handling, and partial output extraction. Two async systems (ACP + interaction chain) must coordinate correctly.

userStories.title: CLI flag wiring — nax plan and nax run --plan
userStories.description:
Wire the new CLI flags for `nax plan` and `nax run --plan` commands.

Changes:

1. Update `nax plan` CLI definition (likely in `src/cli/index.ts` or similar CLI router):
   - `-f, --feature <name>` (required)
   - `--from <spec-path>` (required)
   - `--auto` (optional, default false)
   - `-b, --branch <name>` (optional, default `feat/<feature>`)
   - `-d, --dir <path>` (optional, default cwd)
   - Remove old positional `<description>` arg — if someone passes it, print migration error: "Positional args removed in plan v2. Use: nax plan -f <feature> --from <spec>"

2. Add `--plan` and `--from` flags to `nax run`:
   - `--plan` (boolean, optional)
   - `--from <spec-path>` (required when `--plan` is used)
   - When `--plan` is set: run plan phase first, then confirmation gate, then normal run

3. Confirmation gate for `nax run --plan`:
   - After plan phase completes, display story breakdown summary
   - Wait for user confirmation (Y/n) unless `--headless`
   - In `--headless` mode, skip confirmation and execute immediately

4. Validation: `--from` must point to an existing file. `--plan` without `--from` is an error.
acceptanceCriteria: AC-1: nax plan -f <feature> --from <spec> --auto works end-to-end
AC-2: nax plan -f <feature> --from <spec> starts interactive mode
AC-3: nax plan <description> (old form) prints migration error
AC-4: nax run -f <feature> --plan --from <spec> runs plan then execute
AC-5: Confirmation gate displays story breakdown and waits for Y/n
AC-6: --headless skips confirmation gate
AC-7: --from without existing file throws clear error
AC-8: --plan without --from throws clear error

routing.reasoning: CLI flag parsing and validation. Follows existing patterns in codebase. Main complexity is the confirmation gate UX and the plan-to-run handoff.

userStories.title: PRD JSON validation and schema enforcement
userStories.description:
Create a robust JSON validation layer for LLM-generated PRD output.

New file: `src/prd/schema.ts`

This module validates and normalizes the JSON output from the planning LLM to ensure it conforms to the `PRD` type before writing to disk.

1. `validatePlanOutput(raw: unknown, feature: string, branch: string): PRD` — main validation function:
   - Parse if string (handle markdown code block wrapping: extract from ```json ... ```)
   - Validate required top-level fields: `userStories` (array, non-empty)
   - Auto-fill: `project` (from param or detect), `feature`, `branchName`, `createdAt`, `updatedAt`
   - Per-story validation:
     a. `id` — must be non-empty, alphanumeric with hyphens (validate via `validateStoryId`)
     b. `title` — must be non-empty string
     c. `description` — must be non-empty string
     d. `acceptanceCriteria` — must be non-empty array of strings
     e. `complexity` — must be one of: simple, medium, complex, expert
     f. `testStrategy` — must be valid TestStrategy enum value
     g. `dependencies` — array of story IDs, all must reference valid IDs in same PRD
     h. Force `status: "pending"`, `passes: false`, `attempts: 0`, `escalations: []`
   - Auto-fix common LLM issues:
     a. Strip trailing commas in JSON
     b. Normalize story ID format (e.g., `ST001` → `ST-001`)
     c. Map close-enough complexity strings (e.g., `"Simple"` → `"simple"`)
   - Return validated `PRD` object or throw with specific field-level errors

2. `extractJsonFromMarkdown(text: string): string` — extract JSON from markdown code blocks (```json ... ``` or ``` ... ```).

3. Export for use by both `--auto` and interactive modes.
acceptanceCriteria: AC-1: Valid JSON with all required fields passes validation
AC-2: Missing required fields (id, title, description, acceptanceCriteria) throw with field name in error
AC-3: Invalid complexity values throw with valid options listed
AC-4: Dependency references to non-existent story IDs throw
AC-5: Status is always forced to 'pending' regardless of LLM output
AC-6: JSON wrapped in markdown code blocks is extracted correctly
AC-7: Common LLM quirks (trailing commas, case-insensitive complexity) are auto-fixed
AC-8: Invalid JSON throws with parse error context (line/column if available)

routing.reasoning: Pure validation logic with well-defined inputs/outputs. Multiple edge cases to handle (LLM quirks) but no external dependencies. TDD-lite is ideal — write validation tests first, then implement.

userStories.title: Deprecate nax analyze with migration path
userStories.description:
Deprecate `nax analyze` in favor of `nax plan`, keeping backward compatibility.

Changes:

1. In the CLI router, keep `nax analyze` as a registered command but:
   - Print deprecation warning: `⚠️  'nax analyze' is deprecated. Use 'nax plan -f <feature> --from <spec> --auto' instead.`
   - Then execute the existing analyze logic (no behavior change)
   - Warning goes to stderr so it doesn't break piped output

2. Update `nax plan <description>` (old positional form):
   - Print migration error: `❌ Positional arguments removed in plan v2. Use: nax plan -f <feature> --from <spec.md>`
   - Exit with code 1

3. Update `nax init` scaffolding messages to reference `nax plan` instead of `nax analyze`.

4. Update help text for both commands.
acceptanceCriteria: AC-1: nax analyze still works but prints deprecation warning to stderr
AC-2: nax analyze behavior is unchanged (same output, same exit codes)
AC-3: nax plan <description> (old positional form) prints migration error and exits 1
AC-4: nax init references nax plan in scaffolding messages
AC-5: nax help shows analyze as deprecated

routing.reasoning: Straightforward deprecation wiring. Warning messages and help text changes. No architectural changes.
